evals.prompt: Check my site for keyword cannibalization using the latest GSC export.
evals.expected_output: Should locate the newest gsc-*.json export, read queryPages, group by query, flag queries where two or more pages compete, score severity, and recommend a fix per conflict. Saves a dated JSON report.
assertions: Reads queryPages from the GSC export (or instructs to re-run npm run fetch-gsc if missing)
Flags queries with two or more distinct competing pages
Assigns a severity tag (high / medium / low) per conflict
Gives a rule-based recommendation (consolidate, canonical, or de-optimize)

evals.prompt: Run the cannibalization detector on the demo data and show me the worst conflict.
evals.expected_output: Should run detect.py --demo against the bundled fixture, print a table summary, and surface the highest-severity conflict (the evenly-split 'seo audit checklist' query).
assertions: Runs with the --demo flag against sample_gsc.json
Prints competing URLs with clicks, impressions, and position
Identifies the strongest URL by clicks then impressions then position
Orders conflicts by severity score descending
Does not call the NodesHub API

evals.prompt: I have two pages ranking for 'best running shoes'. Confirm which one actually ranks live and tell me what to do.
evals.expected_output: Should detect the conflict from GSC data, run --verify-serp to confirm the live ranking URL (1 token), and recommend consolidating, canonicalizing, or de-optimizing toward the strongest page.
assertions: Triggers on 'two pages ranking for the same query' phrasing
Uses --verify-serp to confirm the live ranker
Notes the 1 token per query cost of verification
Skips gracefully if no NodesHub key is configured
Recommends a concrete consolidation action toward the strongest URL
